nameMeanings.name: Pembridge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Pembridge (Herefs), which is recorded as Penebrug(g)e from the 11th to 14th centuries. The place-name derives from an Old English personal name * Pena + Old English brycg ‘bridge’. In some cases the surname may be from Pen Bridge in Shipley (Sussex), which derives from an uncertain first element + Old English brycg.

variants.name: Pember
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Pamber, a habitational name from a place in Hampshire named Pamber, from Old English penn ‘fold, enclosure’ + beorg ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Pembridge was born on 1 October 1405, in Weston Subedge, Gloucestershire, England, his father, Thomas PEMBRIDGE, was 17 and his mother, Isabel JOYCE, was 16. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Catherine Bromwich. He died on 1 January 1470, in Weston Subedge, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64.

ko.lifeSummary: John Pembridge(이)가 1405년 10월 1일 영국 그루스스터실르 웨스턴 수베지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas PEMBRIDGE(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel JOYCE(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Catherine Bromwich(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1470년 1월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 웨스턴 수베지에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.
